'ja'} * {'text': '東京都多摩市一ノ宮', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小野神社 (東京都府中市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11464226}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ono Shrine |画像 = [[Image:Fuchu-Ono-jinja haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Front of the shrine |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|51.18|N|139|27|22.98|E|region:JP|name=Ono Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}}{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=370}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）{{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} 18 |本殿 = |別名 = Ono-no-miya |札所等= |例祭 = Sunday in mid-September |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Ono jinja in Fuchu, Tokyo.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Precincts]] '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Ono-no-miya'''. === Overview === It is one of the candidates for the shikinaisha “Ono Shrine” in Tama District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Another candidate is {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}} (for details as a shikinaisha, see {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)#History of shikinaisha “Ono Shrine”|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|1=Ono Shrine}}). Because this shrine is below the {{ill|lt=hake escarpment|Q11275384|WD=Q11275384|1=hake escarpment}}, it is said there came to be two shrines due to relocations caused by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}}; another view says one is the original shrine and the other a branch. While Ono Shrine in Tama was protected by the {{ill|lt=Edo shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}} as the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Musashi Province, this shrine declined as reverence increased for nearby Musashi provincial {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}}, Rokusho-no-miya ({{ill|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine|Q611678|WD=Q611678|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}}). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as gōsha. The shrine burned in a fire in Taishō 15, and the current buildings were rebuilt afterward. === Enshrined deities === Main deities are the following two. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Nakagawara Station|Q4312482|WD=Q4312482|1=Nakagawara Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keio Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Keio Corporation|Q1332506|WD=Q1332506|1=Keio Corporation}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Tokyo'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ono Shrine entry for Fuchū. * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/189}}|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|editor=Saitō Chōshū|volume=2|series=Yūhōdō Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3, Tenki Section, Ono Shrine|pages=368-370|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei|Q11435652|WD=Q11435652|1=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei}}|volume=Vol. 8 {{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|lt=Shinpen Musashi no Kuni Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}} 4|chapter=Vol. 91 Tama District 3, Honjuku Village Ono Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1214848/174}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yūzankaku|Q11659206|WD=Q11659206|1=Yuzankaku}}|editor={{ill|lt=Ashida Ito|Q17988362|WD=Q17988362|1=Koreto Ashida}}|date=1929-8|page=335-337}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Ichinomiya, {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}}) - the other candidate shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Machida)|Q11464227|WD=Q11464227|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Onoji-machi, {{ill|lt=Machida|Q210628|WD=Q210628|1=Machida, Tokyo}}) - enshrined along the Kamakura Road ({{ill|lt=Onoji|Q11464275|WD=Q11464275|1=Onoji}}) toward Ono-go in Musashi Province {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おのしんしやふちゆうし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[category:東京都府中市の歴史]] [[Category:東京都府中市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} 18 |本殿 = |別名 = Ono-no-miya |札所等= |例祭 = Sunday in mid-September |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Ono jinja in Fuchu, Tokyo.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Precincts]] '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Ono-no-miya'''. === Overview === It is one of the candidates for the shikinaisha “Ono Shrine” in Tama District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Another candidate is {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}} (for details as a shikinaisha, see {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)#History of shikinaisha “Ono Shrine”|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|1=Ono Shrine}}). Because this shrine is below the {{ill|lt=hake escarpment|Q11275384|WD=Q11275384|1=hake escarpment}}, it is said there came to be two shrines due to relocations caused by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}}; another view says one is the original shrine and the other a branch. While Ono Shrine in Tama was protected by the {{ill|lt=Edo shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}} as the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Musashi Province, this shrine declined as reverence increased for nearby Musashi provincial {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}}, Rokusho-no-miya ({{ill|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine|Q611678|WD=Q611678|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}}). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as gōsha. The shrine burned in a fire in Taishō 15, and the current buildings were rebuilt afterward. === Enshrined deities === Main deities are the following two. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Nakagawara Station|Q4312482|WD=Q4312482|1=Nakagawara Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keio Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Keio Corporation|Q1332506|WD=Q1332506|1=Keio Corporation}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Tokyo'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ono Shrine entry for Fuchū. * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/189}}|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|editor=Saitō Chōshū|volume=2|series=Yūhōdō Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3, Tenki Section, Ono Shrine|pages=368-370|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei|Q11435652|WD=Q11435652|1=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei}}|volume=Vol. 8 {{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|lt=Shinpen Musashi no Kuni Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}} 4|chapter=Vol. 91 Tama District 3, Honjuku Village Ono Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1214848/174}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yūzankaku|Q11659206|WD=Q11659206|1=Yuzankaku}}|editor={{ill|lt=Ashida Ito|Q17988362|WD=Q17988362|1=Koreto Ashida}}|date=1929-8|page=335-337}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Ichinomiya, {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}}) - the other candidate shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Machida)|Q11464227|WD=Q11464227|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Onoji-machi, {{ill|lt=Machida|Q210628|WD=Q210628|1=Machida, Tokyo}}) - enshrined along the Kamakura Road ({{ill|lt=Onoji|Q11464275|WD=Q11464275|1=Onoji}}) toward Ono-go in Musashi Province {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おのしんしやふちゆうし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[category:東京都府中市の歴史]] [[Category:東京都府中市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}}{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=370}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）{{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} 18 |本殿 = |別名 = Ono-no-miya |札所等= |例祭 = Sunday in mid-September |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Ono jinja in Fuchu, Tokyo.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Precincts]] '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Ono-no-miya'''. === Overview === It is one of the candidates for the shikinaisha “Ono Shrine” in Tama District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Another candidate is {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}} (for details as a shikinaisha, see {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)#History of shikinaisha “Ono Shrine”|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|1=Ono Shrine}}). Because this shrine is below the {{ill|lt=hake escarpment|Q11275384|WD=Q11275384|1=hake escarpment}}, it is said there came to be two shrines due to relocations caused by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}}; another view says one is the original shrine and the other a branch. While Ono Shrine in Tama was protected by the {{ill|lt=Edo shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}} as the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Musashi Province, this shrine declined as reverence increased for nearby Musashi provincial {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}}, Rokusho-no-miya ({{ill|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine|Q611678|WD=Q611678|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}}). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as gōsha. The shrine burned in a fire in Taishō 15, and the current buildings were rebuilt afterward. === Enshrined deities === Main deities are the following two. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Nakagawara Station|Q4312482|WD=Q4312482|1=Nakagawara Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keio Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Keio Corporation|Q1332506|WD=Q1332506|1=Keio Corporation}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Tokyo'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ono Shrine entry for Fuchū. * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/189}}|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|editor=Saitō Chōshū|volume=2|series=Yūhōdō Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3, Tenki Section, Ono Shrine|pages=368-370|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei|Q11435652|WD=Q11435652|1=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei}}|volume=Vol. 8 {{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|lt=Shinpen Musashi no Kuni Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}} 4|chapter=Vol. 91 Tama District 3, Honjuku Village Ono Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1214848/174}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yūzankaku|Q11659206|WD=Q11659206|1=Yuzankaku}}|editor={{ill|lt=Ashida Ito|Q17988362|WD=Q17988362|1=Koreto Ashida}}|date=1929-8|page=335-337}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Ichinomiya, {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}}) - the other candidate shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Machida)|Q11464227|WD=Q11464227|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Onoji-machi, {{ill|lt=Machida|Q210628|WD=Q210628|1=Machida, Tokyo}}) - enshrined along the Kamakura Road ({{ill|lt=Onoji|Q11464275|WD=Q11464275|1=Onoji}}) toward Ono-go in Musashi Province {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[category:東京都府中市の歴史]] [[Category:東京都府中市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'ja'} * {'text': '東京都多摩市一ノ宮', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小野神社 (東京都府中市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11464226}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ono Shrine |画像 = [[Image:Fuchu-Ono-jinja haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Front of the shrine |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|51.18|N|139|27|22.98|E|region:JP|name=Ono Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}}{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=370}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）{{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} 18 |本殿 = |別名 = Ono-no-miya |札所等= |例祭 = Sunday in mid-September |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Ono jinja in Fuchu, Tokyo.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Precincts]] '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Ono-no-miya'''. === Overview === It is one of the candidates for the shikinaisha “Ono Shrine” in Tama District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Another candidate is {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}} (for details as a shikinaisha, see {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)#History of shikinaisha “Ono Shrine”|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|1=Ono Shrine}}). Because this shrine is below the {{ill|lt=hake escarpment|Q11275384|WD=Q11275384|1=hake escarpment}}, it is said there came to be two shrines due to relocations caused by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}}; another view says one is the original shrine and the other a branch. While Ono Shrine in Tama was protected by the {{ill|lt=Edo shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}} as the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Musashi Province, this shrine declined as reverence increased for nearby Musashi provincial {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}}, Rokusho-no-miya ({{ill|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine|Q611678|WD=Q611678|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}}). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as gōsha. The shrine burned in a fire in Taishō 15, and the current buildings were rebuilt afterward. === Enshrined deities === Main deities are the following two. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Nakagawara Station|Q4312482|WD=Q4312482|1=Nakagawara Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keio Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Keio Corporation|Q1332506|WD=Q1332506|1=Keio Corporation}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Tokyo'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ono Shrine entry for Fuchū. * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/189}}|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|editor=Saitō Chōshū|volume=2|series=Yūhōdō Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3, Tenki Section, Ono Shrine|pages=368-370|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei|Q11435652|WD=Q11435652|1=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei}}|volume=Vol. 8 {{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|lt=Shinpen Musashi no Kuni Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}} 4|chapter=Vol. 91 Tama District 3, Honjuku Village Ono Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1214848/174}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yūzankaku|Q11659206|WD=Q11659206|1=Yuzankaku}}|editor={{ill|lt=Ashida Ito|Q17988362|WD=Q17988362|1=Koreto Ashida}}|date=1929-8|page=335-337}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Ichinomiya, {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}}) - the other candidate shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Machida)|Q11464227|WD=Q11464227|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Onoji-machi, {{ill|lt=Machida|Q210628|WD=Q210628|1=Machida, Tokyo}}) - enshrined along the Kamakura Road ({{ill|lt=Onoji|Q11464275|WD=Q11464275|1=Onoji}}) toward Ono-go in Musashi Province {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[category:東京都府中市の歴史]] [[Category:東京都府中市の建築物]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}}{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=370}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）{{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} 18 |本殿 = |別名 = Ono-no-miya |札所等= |例祭 = Sunday in mid-September |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Ono jinja in Fuchu, Tokyo.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Precincts]] '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Ono-no-miya'''. === Overview === It is one of the candidates for the shikinaisha “Ono Shrine” in Tama District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Another candidate is {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}} (for details as a shikinaisha, see {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)#History of shikinaisha “Ono Shrine”|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|1=Ono Shrine}}). Because this shrine is below the {{ill|lt=hake escarpment|Q11275384|WD=Q11275384|1=hake escarpment}}, it is said there came to be two shrines due to relocations caused by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}}; another view says one is the original shrine and the other a branch. While Ono Shrine in Tama was protected by the {{ill|lt=Edo shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}} as the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Musashi Province, this shrine declined as reverence increased for nearby Musashi provincial {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}}, Rokusho-no-miya ({{ill|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine|Q611678|WD=Q611678|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}}). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as gōsha. The shrine burned in a fire in Taishō 15, and the current buildings were rebuilt afterward. === Enshrined deities === Main deities are the following two. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Nakagawara Station|Q4312482|WD=Q4312482|1=Nakagawara Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keio Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Keio Corporation|Q1332506|WD=Q1332506|1=Keio Corporation}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Tokyo'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ono Shrine entry for Fuchū. * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/189}}|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|editor=Saitō Chōshū|volume=2|series=Yūhōdō Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3, Tenki Section, Ono Shrine|pages=368-370|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei|Q11435652|WD=Q11435652|1=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei}}|volume=Vol. 8 {{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|lt=Shinpen Musashi no Kuni Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}} 4|chapter=Vol. 91 Tama District 3, Honjuku Village Ono Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1214848/174}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yūzankaku|Q11659206|WD=Q11659206|1=Yuzankaku}}|editor={{ill|lt=Ashida Ito|Q17988362|WD=Q17988362|1=Koreto Ashida}}|date=1929-8|page=335-337}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Ichinomiya, {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}}) - the other candidate shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Machida)|Q11464227|WD=Q11464227|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Onoji-machi, {{ill|lt=Machida|Q210628|WD=Q210628|1=Machida, Tokyo}}) - enshrined along the Kamakura Road ({{ill|lt=Onoji|Q11464275|WD=Q11464275|1=Onoji}}) toward Ono-go in Musashi Province {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:東京都府中市の歴史]] [[Category:東京都府中市の建築物]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

While Ono Shrine in Tama was protected by the {{ill|lt=Edo shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}} as the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Musashi Province, this shrine declined as reverence increased for nearby Musashi provincial {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}}, Rokusho-no-miya ({{ill|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine|Q611678|WD=Q611678|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}}). The shrine burned in a fire in Taishō 15, and the current buildings were rebuilt afterward. === Enshrined deities === Main deities are the following two. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Nakagawara Station|Q4312482|WD=Q4312482|1=Nakagawara Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keio Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Keio Corporation|Q1332506|WD=Q1332506|1=Keio Corporation}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Tokyo'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ono Shrine entry for Fuchū. * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/189}}|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|editor=Saitō Chōshū|volume=2|series=Yūhōdō Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3, Tenki Section, Ono Shrine|pages=368-370|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei|Q11435652|WD=Q11435652|1=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei}}|volume=Vol. 8 {{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|lt=Shinpen Musashi no Kuni Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}} 4|chapter=Vol. 91 Tama District 3, Honjuku Village Ono Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1214848/174}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yūzankaku|Q11659206|WD=Q11659206|1=Yuzankaku}}|editor={{ill|lt=Ashida Ito|Q17988362|WD=Q17988362|1=Koreto Ashida}}|date=1929-8|page=335-337}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Ichinomiya, {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}}) - {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}}{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=370}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）{{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} 18 |本殿 = |別名 = Ono-no-miya |札所等= |例祭 = Sunday in mid-September |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Ono jinja in Fuchu, Tokyo.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Precincts]] '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Ono-no-miya'''. === Overview === It is one of the candidates for the shikinaisha “Ono Shrine” in Tama District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Another candidate is {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}} (for details as a shikinaisha, see {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)#History of shikinaisha “Ono Shrine”|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|1=Ono Shrine}}). Because this shrine is below the {{ill|lt=hake escarpment|Q11275384|WD=Q11275384|1=hake escarpment}}, it is said there came to be two shrines due to relocations caused by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}}; another view says one is the original shrine and the other a branch. While Ono Shrine in Tama was protected by the {{ill|lt=Edo shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}} as the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Musashi Province, this shrine declined as reverence increased for nearby Musashi provincial {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}}, Rokusho-no-miya ({{ill|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine|Q611678|WD=Q611678|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}}). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as gōsha. The shrine burned in a fire in Taishō 15, and the current buildings were rebuilt afterward. === Enshrined deities === Main deities are the following two. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Nakagawara Station|Q4312482|WD=Q4312482|1=Nakagawara Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keio Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Keio Corporation|Q1332506|WD=Q1332506|1=Keio Corporation}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Tokyo'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ono Shrine entry for Fuchū. * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/189}}|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|editor=Saitō Chōshū|volume=2|series=Yūhōdō Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3, Tenki Section, Ono Shrine|pages=368-370|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei|Q11435652|WD=Q11435652|1=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei}}|volume=Vol. 8 {{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|lt=Shinpen Musashi no Kuni Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}} 4|chapter=Vol. 91 Tama District 3, Honjuku Village Ono Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1214848/174}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yūzankaku|Q11659206|WD=Q11659206|1=Yuzankaku}}|editor={{ill|lt=Ashida Ito|Q17988362|WD=Q17988362|1=Koreto Ashida}}|date=1929-8|page=335-337}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Ichinomiya, {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}}) - the other candidate shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Machida)|Q11464227|WD=Q11464227|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Onoji-machi, {{ill|lt=Machida|Q210628|WD=Q210628|1=Machida, Tokyo}}) - enshrined along the Kamakura Road ({{ill|lt=Onoji|Q11464275|WD=Q11464275|1=Onoji}}) toward Ono-go in Musashi Province {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:東京都府中市の歴史]] [[Category:東京都府中市の建築物]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039727|1=Wono Shrine (Q135039727)}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都府中市住吉町3丁目19-3', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '東京都多摩市一ノ宮', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小野神社 (東京都府中市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11464226}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ono Shrine |画像 = [[Image:Fuchu-Ono-jinja haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Front of the shrine |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|51.18|N|139|27|22.98|E|region:JP|name=Ono Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}}{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=370}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）{{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} 18 |本殿 = |別名 = Ono-no-miya |札所等= |例祭 = Sunday in mid-September |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Ono jinja in Fuchu, Tokyo.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Precincts]] '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Ono-no-miya'''. === Overview === It is one of the candidates for the shikinaisha “Ono Shrine” in Tama District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Another candidate is {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}} (for details as a shikinaisha, see {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)#History of shikinaisha “Ono Shrine”|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|1=Ono Shrine}}). Because this shrine is below the {{ill|lt=hake escarpment|Q11275384|WD=Q11275384|1=hake escarpment}}, it is said there came to be two shrines due to relocations caused by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}}; another view says one is the original shrine and the other a branch. While Ono Shrine in Tama was protected by the {{ill|lt=Edo shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}} as the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Musashi Province, this shrine declined as reverence increased for nearby Musashi provincial {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}}, Rokusho-no-miya ({{ill|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine|Q611678|WD=Q611678|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}}). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as gōsha. The shrine burned in a fire in Taishō 15, and the current buildings were rebuilt afterward. === Enshrined deities === Main deities are the following two. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Nakagawara Station|Q4312482|WD=Q4312482|1=Nakagawara Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keio Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Keio Corporation|Q1332506|WD=Q1332506|1=Keio Corporation}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Tokyo'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ono Shrine entry for Fuchū. * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/189}}|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|editor=Saitō Chōshū|volume=2|series=Yūhōdō Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3, Tenki Section, Ono Shrine|pages=368-370|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei|Q11435652|WD=Q11435652|1=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei}}|volume=Vol. 8 {{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|lt=Shinpen Musashi no Kuni Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}} 4|chapter=Vol. 91 Tama District 3, Honjuku Village Ono Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1214848/174}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yūzankaku|Q11659206|WD=Q11659206|1=Yuzankaku}}|editor={{ill|lt=Ashida Ito|Q17988362|WD=Q17988362|1=Koreto Ashida}}|date=1929-8|page=335-337}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Ichinomiya, {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}}) - the other candidate shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Machida)|Q11464227|WD=Q11464227|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Onoji-machi, {{ill|lt=Machida|Q210628|WD=Q210628|1=Machida, Tokyo}}) - enshrined along the Kamakura Road ({{ill|lt=Onoji|Q11464275|WD=Q11464275|1=Onoji}}) toward Ono-go in Musashi Province {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:東京都府中市の歴史]] [[Category:東京都府中市の建築物]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Front of the shrine |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|51.18|N|139|27|22.98|E|region:JP|name=Ono Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}}{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=370}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）{{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} 18 |本殿 = |別名 = Ono-no-miya |札所等= |例祭 = Sunday in mid-September |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Ono jinja in Fuchu, Tokyo.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Precincts]] '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Ono-no-miya'''. === Overview === It is one of the candidates for the shikinaisha “Ono Shrine” in Tama District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Another candidate is {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}} (for details as a shikinaisha, see {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)#History of shikinaisha “Ono Shrine”|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|1=Ono Shrine}}). Because this shrine is below the {{ill|lt=hake escarpment|Q11275384|WD=Q11275384|1=hake escarpment}}, it is said there came to be two shrines due to relocations caused by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}}; another view says one is the original shrine and the other a branch. While Ono Shrine in Tama was protected by the {{ill|lt=Edo shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}} as the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Musashi Province, this shrine declined as reverence increased for nearby Musashi provincial {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}}, Rokusho-no-miya ({{ill|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine|Q611678|WD=Q611678|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}}). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as gōsha. The shrine burned in a fire in Taishō 15, and the current buildings were rebuilt afterward. === Enshrined deities === Main deities are the following two. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Nakagawara Station|Q4312482|WD=Q4312482|1=Nakagawara Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keio Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Keio Corporation|Q1332506|WD=Q1332506|1=Keio Corporation}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Tokyo'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ono Shrine entry for Fuchū. * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/189}}|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|editor=Saitō Chōshū|volume=2|series=Yūhōdō Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3, Tenki Section, Ono Shrine|pages=368-370|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei|Q11435652|WD=Q11435652|1=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei}}|volume=Vol. 8 {{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|lt=Shinpen Musashi no Kuni Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}} 4|chapter=Vol. 91 Tama District 3, Honjuku Village Ono Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1214848/174}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yūzankaku|Q11659206|WD=Q11659206|1=Yuzankaku}}|editor={{ill|lt=Ashida Ito|Q17988362|WD=Q17988362|1=Koreto Ashida}}|date=1929-8|page=335-337}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Ichinomiya, {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}}) - the other candidate shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Machida)|Q11464227|WD=Q11464227|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Onoji-machi, {{ill|lt=Machida|Q210628|WD=Q210628|1=Machida, Tokyo}}) - enshrined along the Kamakura Road ({{ill|lt=Onoji|Q11464275|WD=Q11464275|1=Onoji}}) toward Ono-go in Musashi Province {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:東京都府中市の歴史]] [[Category:東京都府中市の建築物]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:moved from a redlink]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Ono-no-miya'''. === Overview === It is one of the candidates for the shikinaisha “Ono Shrine” in Tama District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Another candidate is {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}} (for details as a shikinaisha, see {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)#History of shikinaisha “Ono Shrine”|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|1=Ono Shrine}}). Because this shrine is below the {{ill|lt=hake escarpment|Q11275384|WD=Q11275384|1=hake escarpment}}, it is said there came to be two shrines due to relocations caused by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}}; another view says one is the original shrine and the other a branch. While Ono Shrine in Tama was protected by the {{ill|lt=Edo shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}} as the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Musashi Province, this shrine declined as reverence increased for nearby Musashi provincial {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}}, Rokusho-no-miya ({{ill|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine|Q611678|WD=Q611678|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}}). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as gōsha. The shrine burned in a fire in Taishō 15, and the current buildings were rebuilt afterward. === Enshrined deities === Main deities are the following two. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Nakagawara Station|Q4312482|WD=Q4312482|1=Nakagawara Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keio Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Keio Corporation|Q1332506|WD=Q1332506|1=Keio Corporation}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Tokyo'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ono Shrine entry for Fuchū. * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/189}}|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|editor=Saitō Chōshū|volume=2|series=Yūhōdō Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3, Tenki Section, Ono Shrine|pages=368-370|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei|Q11435652|WD=Q11435652|1=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei}}|volume=Vol. 8 {{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|lt=Shinpen Musashi no Kuni Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}} 4|chapter=Vol. 91 Tama District 3, Honjuku Village Ono Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1214848/174}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yūzankaku|Q11659206|WD=Q11659206|1=Yuzankaku}}|editor={{ill|lt=Ashida Ito|Q17988362|WD=Q17988362|1=Koreto Ashida}}|date=1929-8|page=335-337}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Ichinomiya, {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}}) - the other candidate shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Machida)|Q11464227|WD=Q11464227|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Onoji-machi, {{ill|lt=Machida|Q210628|WD=Q210628|1=Machida, Tokyo}}) - enshrined along the Kamakura Road ({{ill|lt=Onoji|Q11464275|WD=Q11464275|1=Onoji}}) toward Ono-go in Musashi Province {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:東京都府中市の歴史]] [[Category:東京都府中市の建築物]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

Front of the shrine |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|51.18|N|139|27|22.98|E|region:JP|name=Ono Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}}{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=370}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）{{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} 18 |本殿 = |別名 = Ono-no-miya |札所等= |例祭 = Sunday in mid-September |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Ono jinja in Fuchu, Tokyo.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Precincts]] '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Ono-no-miya'''. === Overview === It is one of the candidates for the shikinaisha “Ono Shrine” in Tama District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Another candidate is {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}} (for details as a shikinaisha, see {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)#History of shikinaisha “Ono Shrine”|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|1=Ono Shrine}}). Because this shrine is below the {{ill|lt=hake escarpment|Q11275384|WD=Q11275384|1=hake escarpment}}, it is said there came to be two shrines due to relocations caused by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}}; another view says one is the original shrine and the other a branch. While Ono Shrine in Tama was protected by the {{ill|lt=Edo shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}} as the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Musashi Province, this shrine declined as reverence increased for nearby Musashi provincial {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}}, Rokusho-no-miya ({{ill|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine|Q611678|WD=Q611678|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}}). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as gōsha. The shrine burned in a fire in Taishō 15, and the current buildings were rebuilt afterward. === Enshrined deities === Main deities are the following two. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Nakagawara Station|Q4312482|WD=Q4312482|1=Nakagawara Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keio Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Keio Corporation|Q1332506|WD=Q1332506|1=Keio Corporation}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Tokyo'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ono Shrine entry for Fuchū. * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/189}}|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|editor=Saitō Chōshū|volume=2|series=Yūhōdō Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3, Tenki Section, Ono Shrine|pages=368-370|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei|Q11435652|WD=Q11435652|1=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei}}|volume=Vol. 8 {{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|lt=Shinpen Musashi no Kuni Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}} 4|chapter=Vol. 91 Tama District 3, Honjuku Village Ono Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1214848/174}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yūzankaku|Q11659206|WD=Q11659206|1=Yuzankaku}}|editor={{ill|lt=Ashida Ito|Q17988362|WD=Q17988362|1=Koreto Ashida}}|date=1929-8|page=335-337}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Ichinomiya, {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}}) - the other candidate shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Machida)|Q11464227|WD=Q11464227|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Onoji-machi, {{ill|lt=Machida|Q210628|WD=Q210628|1=Machida, Tokyo}}) - enshrined along the Kamakura Road ({{ill|lt=Onoji|Q11464275|WD=Q11464275|1=Onoji}}) toward Ono-go in Musashi Province {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:東京都府中市の歴史]] [[Category:東京都府中市の建築物]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

{{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}}{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=370}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）{{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} 18 |本殿 = |別名 = Ono-no-miya |札所等= |例祭 = Sunday in mid-September |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Ono jinja in Fuchu, Tokyo.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Precincts]] '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Ono-no-miya'''. === Overview === It is one of the candidates for the shikinaisha “Ono Shrine” in Tama District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Another candidate is {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}} (for details as a shikinaisha, see {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)#History of shikinaisha “Ono Shrine”|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|1=Ono Shrine}}). Because this shrine is below the {{ill|lt=hake escarpment|Q11275384|WD=Q11275384|1=hake escarpment}}, it is said there came to be two shrines due to relocations caused by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}}; another view says one is the original shrine and the other a branch. While Ono Shrine in Tama was protected by the {{ill|lt=Edo shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}} as the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Musashi Province, this shrine declined as reverence increased for nearby Musashi provincial {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}}, Rokusho-no-miya ({{ill|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine|Q611678|WD=Q611678|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}}). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as gōsha. The shrine burned in a fire in Taishō 15, and the current buildings were rebuilt afterward. === Enshrined deities === Main deities are the following two. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Nakagawara Station|Q4312482|WD=Q4312482|1=Nakagawara Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keio Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Keio Corporation|Q1332506|WD=Q1332506|1=Keio Corporation}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Tokyo'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ono Shrine entry for Fuchū. * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/189}}|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|editor=Saitō Chōshū|volume=2|series=Yūhōdō Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3, Tenki Section, Ono Shrine|pages=368-370|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei|Q11435652|WD=Q11435652|1=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei}}|volume=Vol. 8 {{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|lt=Shinpen Musashi no Kuni Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}} 4|chapter=Vol. 91 Tama District 3, Honjuku Village Ono Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1214848/174}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yūzankaku|Q11659206|WD=Q11659206|1=Yuzankaku}}|editor={{ill|lt=Ashida Ito|Q17988362|WD=Q17988362|1=Koreto Ashida}}|date=1929-8|page=335-337}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Ichinomiya, {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}}) - the other candidate shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Machida)|Q11464227|WD=Q11464227|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Onoji-machi, {{ill|lt=Machida|Q210628|WD=Q210628|1=Machida, Tokyo}}) - enshrined along the Kamakura Road ({{ill|lt=Onoji|Q11464275|WD=Q11464275|1=Onoji}}) toward Ono-go in Musashi Province {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:東京都府中市の歴史]] [[Category:東京都府中市の建築物]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

{{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}}{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=370}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）{{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} 18 |本殿 = |別名 = Ono-no-miya |札所等= |例祭 = Sunday in mid-September |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Ono jinja in Fuchu, Tokyo.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Precincts]] '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Ono-no-miya'''. === Overview === It is one of the candidates for the shikinaisha “Ono Shrine” in Tama District, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Another candidate is {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}} (for details as a shikinaisha, see {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)#History of shikinaisha “Ono Shrine”|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|1=Ono Shrine}}). Because this shrine is below the {{ill|lt=hake escarpment|Q11275384|WD=Q11275384|1=hake escarpment}}, it is said there came to be two shrines due to relocations caused by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}}; another view says one is the original shrine and the other a branch. While Ono Shrine in Tama was protected by the {{ill|lt=Edo shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}} as the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Musashi Province, this shrine declined as reverence increased for nearby Musashi provincial {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}}, Rokusho-no-miya ({{ill|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine|Q611678|WD=Q611678|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}}). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as gōsha. The shrine burned in a fire in Taishō 15, and the current buildings were rebuilt afterward. === Enshrined deities === Main deities are the following two. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Shitaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|WD=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} Sumiyoshi-chō 3-19-3 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Nakagawara Station|Q4312482|WD=Q4312482|1=Nakagawara Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keio Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Keio Corporation|Q1332506|WD=Q1332506|1=Keio Corporation}} (about 8 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Tokyo'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ono Shrine entry for Fuchū. * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/189}}|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|editor=Saitō Chōshū|volume=2|series=Yūhōdō Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3, Tenki Section, Ono Shrine|pages=368-370|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei|Q11435652|WD=Q11435652|1=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei}}|volume=Vol. 8 {{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|lt=Shinpen Musashi no Kuni Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}} 4|chapter=Vol. 91 Tama District 3, Honjuku Village Ono Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1214848/174}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yūzankaku|Q11659206|WD=Q11659206|1=Yuzankaku}}|editor={{ill|lt=Ashida Ito|Q17988362|WD=Q17988362|1=Koreto Ashida}}|date=1929-8|page=335-337}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Tama)|Q11464224|WD=Q11464224|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Ichinomiya, {{ill|lt=Tama|Q213263|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}}) - the other candidate shikinaisha * {{ill|lt=Ono Shrine (Machida)|Q11464227|WD=Q11464227|lt=Ono Shrine|1=Ono Shrine}} (Onoji-machi, {{ill|lt=Machida|Q210628|WD=Q210628|1=Machida, Tokyo}}) - enshrined along the Kamakura Road ({{ill|lt=Onoji|Q11464275|WD=Q11464275|1=Onoji}}) toward Ono-go in Musashi Province {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Fuchū)]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:東京都府中市の歴史]] [[Category:東京都府中市の建築物]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]]
